decisions.title: Should Sift Edge sync-to-Sift be prioritized over Edge PRD completion?
decisions.context: ENG-8855 (Edge Sync prototype) and ENG-8149 (Edge PRD update) are both active. Edge is a Build the Moat initiative, but unclear if we should prototype before finalizing strategy docs.

arguments.for: Prototype validates technical feasibility early, reduces risk of spec-building without proof of concept, accelerates learning
arguments.against: PRD-first ensures strategic alignment, prevents building wrong thing, Edge RFC (ENG-9074) needs clear requirements

decisions.title: Invest in calculated channel error handling now or defer for Explore V2 GA?
decisions.context: ENG-8965 (resolver errors) and ENG-9069 (expression validation errors) suggest calculated channels have production issues. Core to 'Nail the Core' pillar but may distract from Explore V2 launch.

arguments.for: Data quality is foundational to Signal>Evidence>Decision; errors break trust; impacts Decisions Captured metric if users can't rely on calcs
arguments.against: Explore V2 launch is imminent commitment; can address in V2.1; workarounds exist; limited customer escalations so far

decisions.title: Address data-batcher scalability concerns before Canvas beta expansion?
decisions.context: ENG-9050 (multi-pod dashboards), ENG-9049 (observability metrics), and ENG-8228 (MemTable reads) indicate data-batcher scaling work. Canvas success depends on reliable high-volume data ingestion.
decisions.blockedBy: Need Canvas beta usage projections and SLA requirements from Product

arguments.for: Canvas is Embed Deeper pillar; scaling issues will block beta expansion; observability gaps risk production incidents
arguments.against: Current architecture handles load; premature optimization; should wait for real beta usage patterns

decisions.title: Prioritize Python Rules CVE patches or accept security risk?
decisions.context: ENG-8398 (Apache Avro RCE), ENG-8406 (Zookeeper auth bypass), ENG-8408 (crypto/tls) are security vulnerabilities. Python Rules is customer-facing, but patches may be time-consuming.

arguments.for: RCE is critical severity; customer trust is moat-building; compliance requirements; could block enterprise deals
arguments.against: Low exploitability in our deployment model; engineering bandwidth is constrained; can mitigate with network controls

decisions.title: Should Run Create Report polish (ENG-8921) block Explore V2 GA?
decisions.context: Agg Review Alignment is UX polish for run creation workflow. Explore V2 is 'Nail the Core' priority, but feature completeness vs. speed-to-market tradeoff.

arguments.for: Run creation is core workflow; poor UX undermines adoption; feedback from design partners indicates confusion
arguments.against: Can ship as V2.1 enhancement; GA definition should focus on core decision-making capabilities; fast iteration beats perfection

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting load (80% of week) limiting execution capacity. Three security CVEs require immediate attention. Engineering signals show healthy progress on Edge (Build Moat) and calculated channels (Nail Core), but scaling concerns emerging for Canvas beta expansion.
alerts: Security CVE review meeting not scheduled - ENG-8398/8406/8408 require decision by 2026-03-12
Canvas scaling decision (data-batcher) blocked on Product inputs - delaying beta expansion timeline
Explore V2 GA at risk - calculated channel errors and Run Report polish may push deadline
Edge PRD and RFC activities overlapping - potential duplication of effort or misalignment
Only 2 focus blocks today - consider declining non-critical meetings to unblock strategic decisions
